result: Gaston Leroux wrote the novel upon which "The Phantom of the Opera," the longest-running show in Broadway history, is based[1](https://www.hachettebookgroup.com/titles/gaston-leroux/the-phantom-of-the-opera/9781454954590/), [2](https://medium.com/@cwmelzer/the-10-broadway-shows-with-the-longest-runs-6ee84a98e430), [3](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/literature-and-writing/phantom-opera-gaston-leroux), [4](https://www.smithsonianmag.com/smart-news/the-longest-running-show-in-broadway-history-opened-on-this-date-in-1988-read-why-its-legacy-is-so-controversial-180985860/), [5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaston_Leroux), [6](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Gaston-Leroux), [7](https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/480204.The_Phantom_of_the_Opera). The musical, which opened on Broadway on January 26, 1988, is an adaptation of Leroux's 1910 novel of the same name[2](https://medium.com/@cwmelzer/the-10-broadway-shows-with-the-longest-runs-6ee84a98e430), [3](https://www.ebsco.com/research-starters/literature-and-writing/phantom-opera-gaston-leroux), [4](https://www.smithsonianmag.com/smart-news/the-longest-running-show-in-broadway-history-opened-on-this-date-in-1988-read-why-its-legacy-is-so-controversial-180985860/). Leroux, a French journalist and author, is best known for "The Phantom of the Opera"[1](https://www.hachettebookgroup.com/titles/gaston-leroux/the-phantom-of-the-opera/9781454954590/), [5](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gaston_Leroux), [6](https://www.britannica.com/biography/Gaston-Leroux), [7](https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/480204.The_Phantom_of_the_Opera).

direct_output:
**Process Summary:** To address the query about who wrote the novel on which the longest-running show in Broadway history is based, the following steps were taken:
1. Initially, the Wikipedia_RAG_Search_Tool was used to find the longest-running Broadway show and the novel it is based on. However, the relevant pages section did not yield the specific information needed.
2. Subsequently, the Ground_Google_Search_Tool was employed to find the name of the longest-running Broadway show and the novel it is based on. The search results indicated that the novel "The Phantom of the Opera" is the source material for the longest-running Broadway show, which opened on January 26, 1988. The novel was written by Gaston Leroux.

**Answer:** Gaston Leroux wrote the novel on which the longest-running show in Broadway history, "The Phantom of the Opera," is based.
